---
title: "Roadmap"
description: "Milestones, the prioritized issue backlog, and design goals captured early."
---

The strategy is a **vertical slice**: build the hardest, most-differentiating piece
(a tactical battle) first, make it fun, then grow outward into a campaign.

## Milestones

| Milestone | Scope | Status |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **M1** | One playable, self-contained tactical battle. | ✅ Scaffolded & playable |
| **M2** | CK3-style **campaign map** --- clickable provinces, characters/realms, turn-based diplomacy & war. Battles **auto-resolved** at first. | Not started |
| **M3** | **Integration** --- an army battle on the campaign map launches into the M1 battle scene and returns a result (winner, casualties). Where the two genres meet. | Not started |

The battle scene was kept self-contained specifically to make the M3 hand-off clean.

## Prioritized backlog

Tracked as issues on [`Lacaedemon/sparta`](https://github.com/Lacaedemon/sparta/issues)
with `P0`--`P3` labels. Order reflects **dependencies** --- validate the foundation, build
the collision pillar, then features that depend on it, then independent polish.

### P0 --- Foundation
- First run & verification in Godot --- nothing below is validated until this passes.
- Spacebar active pause --- confirm live.

### P1 --- Collision pillar (in dependency order)
- **Per-type footprint** --- `_separate()` currently uses a shared `RADIUS`; make it per-type.
- **NavigationAgent2D pathfinding** --- decide the path-vs-collision split here.
- **Scale beyond O(n²)** --- pairs with pathfinding.
- **Formation cohesion** --- depends on per-type footprint.
- **Hard blocking** --- spears stop cavalry; depends on solid collision + formations.

### P2 --- Features on the shared "collision-exemption" primitive
Build the primitive once, then reuse it:

- **Friendly pass-through** --- simplest; establishes the primitive.
- **Line relief** --- adds a fatigue stat + a choreographed front-line handoff.
- **Unit merging** --- stat blending + a temporary "strangers" debuff.

### P3 --- Independent polish
- **Richer selection** --- double-click type-select, control groups.

## Design goals captured early

These are recorded so the intent isn't lost --- not yet scheduled.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Unit merging --- combine two units into one
Fold depleted units together after casualties, or deliberately bind units into a
single wider, coordinated block (ties into the collision/formation pillar). Merging
forces strangers together, so the result starts with a **temporary debuff** that
decays as the unit "gels". Open questions: capped vs summed `max_soldiers`, type/team
restrictions, reversibility, and how to blend stats.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Line relief --- cycle tired units out of combat
A fresh unit relieves an engaged friendly: it advances into the front-line slot as
the tired one peels back. Requires a **new fatigue/stamina stat** and a choreographed
swap where the two units **pass through each other** --- directly exercising the
collision-exemption primitive. The hard part is making the handoff read well and not
be exploitable.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Move-through friendly units --- coordinated pass-through
Let a unit move *through* an idle friendly smoothly (ranks interleave, the idle unit
parts and reforms) instead of colliding or detouring. Shares the collision-exemption
mechanism with line relief; distinct in that the mover keeps going rather than taking
over the slot.
:::

Relief, merging, and pass-through should likely share **one** underlying
"soft-pass / collision-exemption" primitive --- which is why P2 builds it once.

---

*Source: [`PLAN.md`](https://github.com/Lacaedemon/sparta/blob/main/PLAN.md) in the
repository. The issue tracker is the live source of truth for status.*
